goal: Land `crates/ucil-treesitter/src/symbols.rs` with a `SymbolExtractor` that, given a `tree_sitter::Tree` + source + `Language`, returns `Vec<ExtractedSymbol>` covering function, method, class, struct, enum, and trait definitions with start/end line ranges and captured signatures. Unblocks P1-W2-F04 (LMDB tag cache) and P1-W4-F04 (tree-sitter ‚Üí knowledge graph pipeline) downstream.
plan_summary: Build on WO-0005's `crates/ucil-treesitter/src/parser.rs` (frozen) ‚Äî which already exposes `Parser::parse(&str, Language) -> Result<tree_sitter::Tree, _>` across 11 grammars ‚Äî by adding a sibling module `symbols.rs`. The new module defines (1) a `SymbolKind` enum (`#[non_exhaustive]`) with variants Function, Method, Class, Struct, Enum, Trait, Interface, TypeAlias, Constant, Module ‚Äî the six primary kinds listed in master-plan ¬ß18 Week 2 plus the minimum headroom for TS/Go/Python interfaces/type-aliases/modules; (2) an `ExtractedSymbol` struct with fields `name: String`, `kind: SymbolKind`, `file_path: PathBuf`, `language: Language` (re-exporting the parser's enum), `start_line: u32`, `start_col: u32`, `end_line: u32`, `end_col: u32`, `signature: Option<String>`, `doc_comment: Option<String>` ‚Äî every field is Serde-derivable; (3) a stateless `SymbolExtractor` (unit struct) with `new() -> Self` and `extract(&self, tree: &tree_sitter::Tree, source: &str, file_path: &Path, lang: Language) -> Vec<ExtractedSymbol>` ‚Äî the single public entry-point dispatches on `lang` into per-language helpers. Per-language helpers use `tree_sitter::Query` + `QueryCursor` with language-specific `.scm`-style query strings stored as `const &str` ‚Äî Rust captures `function_item`/`struct_item`/`enum_item`/`trait_item`/`impl_item` methods; Python captures `function_definition`/`class_definition` (methods are nested function_definition captures with `has_ancestor_kind("class_definition")`); TypeScript/JavaScript share a helper that captures `function_declaration`/`class_declaration`/`method_definition`/`interface_declaration`/`type_alias_declaration`; Go captures `function_declaration`/`method_declaration`/`type_declaration`. Languages not in the primary-five (Java, C, C++, Ruby, Bash, JSON) are handled by a uniform fallback that returns an empty `Vec` so that `extract` never panics for any `Language` variant ‚Äî the panics-free guarantee is a module invariant. Signature capture: for function/method nodes, take the source substring from `node.start_byte()` to the first `{` (or to end-of-declaration for Rust trait methods, heuristically the first `;`) ‚Äî best-effort, always a single line or truncated at 200 chars. Doc-comment capture: for Rust, walk backwards from the symbol's preceding sibling and collect contiguous `line_comment` nodes whose text starts with `///`; for Python, inspect the symbol's body node's first statement and take it if it's an `expression_statement(string)` docstring; for TS/JS, walk backwards for the preceding `comment` node whose text starts with `/**`. All three are opportunistic ‚Äî `None` when no doc-comment found. Module-root tests live flat in `symbols.rs` (NOT wrapped in `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { }`) per DEC-0005 so the frozen nextest selector `symbols::` resolves every test path as `ucil_treesitter::symbols::<test_name>` ‚Äî avoiding the WO-0006 selector-mismatch failure mode.
scope_in: crates/ucil-treesitter/src/symbols.rs (new module, ~450-550 lines incl. tests + per-language query strings)
* `SymbolKind` enum: Function, Method, Class, Struct, Enum, Trait, Interface, TypeAlias, Constant, Module ‚Äî `#[non_exhaustive]`, derives Debug+Clone+Copy+PartialEq+Eq+Hash+Serialize+Deserialize

* `ExtractedSymbol` struct: name, kind, file_path, language, start_line, start_col, end_line, end_col, signature: Option<String>, doc_comment: Option<String> ‚Äî derives Debug+Clone+PartialEq+Eq+Serialize+Deserialize

* `SymbolExtractor` unit struct with `new() -> Self` + `extract(&self, tree: &tree_sitter::Tree, source: &str, file_path: &Path, lang: Language) -> Vec<ExtractedSymbol>` as the single public entry-point

* Per-language helpers (private): `extract_rust`, `extract_python`, `extract_ts_js` (shared by TypeScript + JavaScript, disambiguated by flag), `extract_go` ‚Äî each builds a `tree_sitter::Query` against the language's `LANGUAGE.into()` and iterates matches via `QueryCursor`

* Shared utilities (private): `node_text(Node, &str) -> &str`, `usize_to_u32(usize) -> u32` (clamps via `u32::try_from(..).unwrap_or(u32::MAX)`), `signature_from_node(Node, &str) -> String`, `has_ancestor_kind(Node, &str) -> bool`, `rust_doc_comment_preceding(Node, &str) -> Option<String>` (and analogues for Python / TS)

* Fallback arm: for `Language::Java | Language::C | Language::Cpp | Language::Ruby | Language::Bash | Language::Json` return `Vec::new()` ‚Äî documented in rustdoc as 'minimal support in Phase 1; richer extraction in a future WO'
* `tracing::instrument` (level="debug") on `extract` with span name `ucil.treesitter.extract_symbols` per master-plan ¬ß15.2 (`ucil.<layer>.<op>`) ‚Äî `layer=treesitter`, `op=extract_symbols`
crates/ucil-treesitter/src/lib.rs ‚Äî add `pub mod symbols;` + `pub use symbols::{ExtractedSymbol, SymbolExtractor, SymbolKind};`

crates/ucil-treesitter/Cargo.toml ‚Äî add `serde = { workspace = true, features = ["derive"] }` ONLY if `ExtractedSymbol` / `SymbolKind` derive `Serialize/Deserialize` and the dep is not yet present (check workspace root first; `serde` is almost certainly workspace-available via `ucil-core`'s usage)
Module-root tests in `symbols.rs` (flat ‚Äî NOT under `mod tests { }`, per DEC-0005 to guarantee selector robustness):

* `symbols_extract_rust_fn_and_struct_and_enum` ‚Äî parses `"fn foo() {} struct Bar {} enum Baz { A, B }"` via `Parser::parse(.., Language::Rust)`, calls `SymbolExtractor::new().extract(&tree, source, Path::new("x.rs"), Language::Rust)`, asserts `.len() == 3`, asserts the set of (name, kind) equals `{("foo", Function), ("Bar", Struct), ("Baz", Enum)}`, asserts every `language == Language::Rust`, asserts every `file_path == PathBuf::from("x.rs")`
* `symbols_extract_rust_trait_and_method` ‚Äî parses `"trait T { fn m(&self); } impl T for () { fn m(&self) {} }"`, asserts one `Trait` named `T`, at least one `Method` named `m`
* `symbols_extract_typescript_function_and_class_and_interface` ‚Äî parses `"function foo(){} class B {} interface I { x: number; }"`, asserts Function(foo), Class(B), Interface(I)
* `symbols_extract_javascript_function_declaration` ‚Äî parses `"function foo() { return 1; }"`, asserts exactly one Function(foo)
* `symbols_extract_line_ranges_are_well_formed` ‚Äî on the Rust triple-symbol source, asserts every symbol has `start_line >= 1`, `end_line >= start_line`, `start_col >= 1`, `end_col >= 1`

* `symbols_extract_signature_captures_rust_fn_declaration` ‚Äî parses `"fn add(x: i32, y: i32) -> i32 { x + y }"`, asserts the extracted `add` symbol's `signature` field is `Some` and its string contains `fn add(x: i32, y: i32) -> i32` (exact substring ‚Äî body text must be absent)

* `symbols_extract_doc_comment_for_rust_fn` ‚Äî parses `"/// double a number\nfn dbl(x: i32) -> i32 { x * 2 }"`, asserts the `dbl` symbol's `doc_comment == Some("/// double a number".into())` (or the trimmed body `"double a number"` ‚Äî executor picks one, documents it in rustdoc, test asserts whichever was chosen)
* `symbols_extract_empty_source_returns_empty_vec` ‚Äî `extract` on an empty source for every language in `SUPPORTED_LANGUAGES` returns `Vec::new()`
* `symbols_extract_all_supported_languages_no_panic` ‚Äî iterates `SUPPORTED_LANGUAGES`, parses `"// placeholder"` and calls `extract` on each ‚Äî must not panic, result length is any value ‚â• 0
* `symbols_extract_fallback_languages_return_empty` ‚Äî for Java/C/C++/Ruby/Bash/JSON, `extract` returns `Vec::new()` (documents the scope_out guarantee as a contract test)

* `symbols_extract_language_field_populated_correctly` ‚Äî for each of the 4 primary languages (Rust, Python, TypeScript, Go), parses a minimal symbol and asserts the extracted symbol's `language` equals the input `Language`

Rustdoc on every public item: `SymbolKind`, every `SymbolKind` variant, `ExtractedSymbol`, every `ExtractedSymbol` field, `SymbolExtractor`, `SymbolExtractor::new`, `SymbolExtractor::extract` ‚Äî with a module-level rustdoc that (a) cites master-plan ¬ß18 Phase 1 Week 2 and ¬ß2.1 Layer 1, (b) lists which languages are primary-support vs fallback, (c) documents the signature heuristic (everything before `{` or first `;`), (d) documents the doc-comment heuristic per language
`#![allow(clippy::module_name_repetitions)]` in `symbols.rs` IF (and only if) pedantic flags `ExtractedSymbol`/`SymbolExtractor` inside `symbols` module ‚Äî same escape hatch used by `parser.rs`
`tracing::debug!` per match iteration is acceptable; do NOT open a new span per match (spans are per call to `extract`, not per captured symbol)
scope_out: LMDB tag cache (P1-W2-F04) ‚Äî separate future WO
Any modification to `crates/ucil-treesitter/src/parser.rs` ‚Äî frozen by WO-0005; the `git diff == 0` acceptance check enforces byte-for-byte preservation

Any modification to `crates/ucil-core/src/types.rs::Symbol` ‚Äî `ExtractedSymbol` is a fresh tree-sitter-layer type with extra fields (line range, signature); the bridge to `ucil_core::types::Symbol` (with lossy `end_line` ‚Üí drop) is deferred to P1-W4-F04 (tree-sitter ‚Üí knowledge-graph pipeline) and NOT introduced here
Java, C, C++, Ruby, Bash, JSON full-fidelity extraction ‚Äî these return `Vec::new()` from `extract` in Phase 1; a richer extraction may be written in a later WO
Adding any `ucil-daemon` dependency edge to `ucil-treesitter` ‚Äî phase-log invariant 5 forbids it
Adding new grammar crates ‚Äî the 11 already in `Cargo.toml` are sufficient
Interop / conversion from `ExtractedSymbol` ‚Üí `ucil_core::types::Symbol` ‚Äî reserved for P1-W4-F04

New ADRs ‚Äî the signature & doc-comment heuristics are small enough to document inline in the module's rustdoc; raise a proposed ADR only if the executor discovers a genuine ambiguity (e.g. a tree-sitter ABI version mismatch between the bundled grammars)
acceptance: New `symbols` module exists at `crates/ucil-treesitter/src/symbols.rs` and is declared + re-exported from `lib.rs`
`cargo nextest run -p ucil-treesitter symbols::` matches ‚â• 10 tests, all passing (the P1-W2-F02 acceptance selector)
`cargo clippy -p ucil-treesitter --all-targets -- -D warnings` is clean (pedantic + nursery ‚Äî module inherits the lints from `lib.rs`)
`cargo doc -p ucil-treesitter --no-deps` is clean (no broken intra-doc links)
Reality-check oracle: stashing `symbols.rs` causes the selector `symbols::` to return 0 matching tests (or cargo build fails); popping restores
No mocks of tree-sitter grammars or parsing ‚Äî tests call `Parser::parse(‚Ä¶)` with real source strings and real grammar crates
`Parser` public surface from WO-0005 remains byte-for-byte unchanged (git diff proves it)
`ucil-core` crate remains byte-for-byte unchanged (git diff proves it)
Module-root tests are flat ‚Äî no `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { }` wrapper anywhere in `symbols.rs` ‚Äî per DEC-0005
`extract` does not panic on any `Language` variant (enforced by `symbols_extract_all_supported_languages_no_panic` test)
acceptance_criteria: cargo nextest run -p ucil-treesitter 'symbols::' --status-level=pass --hide-progress-bar 2>&1 | grep -E '^\s+PASS' | awk 'END{exit (NR < 10)}'
context_refs: master-plan:¬ß18 Phase 1 Week 2 (line 1731 ‚Äî 'Implement ucil-treesitter: multi-language parser, symbol extraction, AST-aware chunking')
master-plan:¬ß12.1 Knowledge graph schema ‚Äî symbols table (target shape for future ucil_core::types::Symbol bridge, NOT introduced in this WO)
master-plan:¬ß15.2 Tracing span naming convention (ucil.<layer>.<op>) ‚Äî span name `ucil.treesitter.extract_symbols`

decision:DEC-0005-WO-0006-module-coherence-commits (flat module-root tests for selector robustness; also: single module-introduction commit may exceed the 50-line soft target when splitting would produce dead-code intermediate states)
rules:.claude/rules/rust-style.md (thiserror; pedantic+nursery; tracing; no unwrap outside #[cfg(test)]; module-root rustdoc; edition 2021; one crate per Cargo.toml)

phase-log:ucil-build/phase-log/01-phase-1/CLAUDE.md (invariants 1, 5, 7: no mocks of tree-sitter; ucil-treesitter depends only on ucil-core + tree-sitter crates; tokio::time::timeout on every IO .await ‚Äî not applicable since `extract` is CPU-bound-sync, not async)
lessons_applied: Module-root flat tests (no `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { }` wrapper) per DEC-0005 ‚Äî the selector `symbols::` is a substring/prefix match so it WOULD match a `mod tests` wrapper, but DEC-0005's pattern standardizes on flat placement to avoid the brittleness class that broke P1-W2-F06 (`storage::test_two_tier_layout` ‚Äî exact-test-name selector against a `mod tests`-wrapped test)
No mocking of tree-sitter parsing or grammar loading ‚Äî tests use real `Parser::parse(‚Ä¶)` against real grammar crates (phase-log invariant 1)

thiserror + `#[non_exhaustive]` on any `Error` types; `ExtractedSymbol`/`SymbolKind` are data types, not errors ‚Äî no new error enum is required if `extract` is infallible (the plan here is that `extract` returns `Vec<ExtractedSymbol>` directly, never a `Result`; internal `Query::new` failures are converted to an empty `Vec` + `tracing::warn!` rather than a new typed error, documented in rustdoc)
Do NOT modify `parser.rs` ‚Äî the `git diff == 0` acceptance check enforces byte-for-byte preservation
Do NOT modify any file under `crates/ucil-core/` ‚Äî `ExtractedSymbol` is a fresh tree-sitter-layer type; the bridge to `ucil_core::types::Symbol` is P1-W4-F04 territory
Do NOT add a `ucil-daemon` dependency edge ‚Äî phase-log invariant 5 forbids it; the acceptance check `! grep -q 'ucil-daemon' Cargo.toml` enforces

Signatures heuristic is 'everything before first {' (or `;` for trait method decls); doc-comments heuristic is per-language ‚Äî both documented in module rustdoc; contested edge cases pause and raise a proposed ADR

If the module file exceeds ~400 lines (likely given 11-language coverage + 12 tests), the DEC-0001/DEC-0005 module-introduction-commit precedent applies: a single coherent `feat(treesitter): add SymbolExtractor + SymbolKind + ExtractedSymbol` commit at up to ~550 lines is acceptable if splitting would produce dead-code intermediate states (e.g. an `extract` that returns empty `Vec` until the last language is added)
Conventional Commits with `Phase: 1`, `Feature: P1-W2-F02`, `Work-order: WO-0017` trailers on every commit
Push after every commit; do NOT `--amend` after push; do NOT force-push
